title: Whack-a-Mole 3D with Parrots
prompt:
Create a complete single HTML file implementing a 3D Whack-a-Mole game using Three.js and GLTFLoader. Load the parrot model from 'Parrot.glb' using GLTFLoader.

## Scene Setup
- Create a 3x3 grid of 9 'holes' on a flat green ground plane (positions indexed 0-8, left-to-right, top-to-bottom).
- Each hole is a dark brown cylinder (radius 0.8, height 0.3) embedded in the ground at positions: row = Math.floor(i/3), col = i%3, spaced 3 units apart, centered at origin.
- Camera: PerspectiveCamera at position (0, 12, 10) looking at (0, 0, 0).
- Lights: AmbientLight (0xffffff, 0.5) and DirectionalLight (0xffffff, 0.8) at (5, 10, 5).
- Sky: set renderer clear color to 0x87CEEB.

## Parrot Moles
- Load 'Parrot.glb' once, then clone it 9 times (one per hole).
- Scale each parrot clone to 0.015 uniformly.
- Each parrot starts hidden below its hole (y = -2). When 'popping up', animate y from -2 to 1.0 over 300ms. When hiding, animate y from 1.0 to -2 over 200ms.
- Parrots rotate slowly around Y axis (1 rad/s) when visible.

## Game Logic
- Game has 3 states: 'idle', 'playing', 'gameover'.
- A 'Start Game' button (id='startBtn') begins the game. Game duration is 30 seconds.
- During 'playing', every 1000ms, randomly select 1-3 holes (that don't already have a visible parrot) to pop up parrots. Each parrot stays visible for 2000ms before auto-hiding.
- Difficulty ramp: after 10 seconds, spawn interval decreases to 750ms. After 20 seconds, decreases to 500ms.
- Clicking (raycasting) on a visible parrot: +10 points (score), parrot immediately hides, increment 'whacked' counter. Play a brief scale-up animation (scale to 0.025 then back to 0.015 in 150ms) before hiding.
- Clicking an empty hole or ground: -2 points (score), increment 'misses' counter. Score cannot go below 0.
- Combo system: consecutive hits without a miss multiply points: 1st hit=10, 2nd consecutive=20, 3rd+=30 (capped at 30). A miss resets combo to 0.
- When timer reaches 0, state becomes 'gameover', all parrots hide, show 'Game Over' overlay (id='gameOverOverlay') with final score.
- A 'Restart' button (id='restartBtn') inside the overlay resets everything.

## HUD Elements
- Score display: div id='scoreDisplay' showing 'Score: X'
- Timer display: div id='timerDisplay' showing 'Time: Xs'
- Combo display: div id='comboDisplay' showing 'Combo: Nx'
- Whacked count: div id='whackedDisplay' showing 'Whacked: X'
- Misses count: div id='missesDisplay' showing 'Misses: X'
- All HUD elements positioned at top of screen with CSS.

## Mallet Cursor
- Create a simple mallet from a cylinder (handle) and box (head) as a group. Attach it to a raycaster so it follows the mouse intersection point on the ground plane, hovering at y=3.
- On click, animate the mallet downward (y from 3 to 0.5) and back up over 200ms.

## State Management (CRITICAL)
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ with these fields updated every frame:
- gameState: 'idle' | 'playing' | 'gameover'
- score: number (current score)
- timeRemaining: number (seconds remaining, integer)
- combo: number (current combo multiplier: 0, 1, 2, or 3)
- whacked: number (total parrots whacked)
- misses: number (total misses)
- totalSpawned: number (total parrots that have popped up)
- visibleParrots: array of hole indices (0-8) where parrots are currently visible (y > 0)
- malletY: number (current mallet group y position)
- parrotPositions: array of 9 numbers (y position of each parrot clone)
- spawnInterval: number (current spawn interval in ms)
- modelLoaded: boolean (true once Parrot.glb is loaded)
- holeCount: 9

Use requestAnimationFrame for the game loop. Ensure all physics and positions are NaN-free.
physics_constraints: Parrot Y positions must be clamped between -2 and 1.0. Score cannot go below 0. Combo multiplier capped at 3. Timer counts down from 30 to 0 and stops. Mallet Y oscillates between 0.5 and 3.0 during strike animation. All numerical values must be NaN-free at all times.
